AgentStreamEvent.description:
Agent-loop stream events. Higher-level than `coco_types::StreamEvent` (which represents raw LLM inference deltas). Adds: - Tool lifecycle states (Queued ‚Üí Started ‚Üí Completed) - MCP tool call tracking - Turn-scoped item IDs

Input to `StreamAccumulator`. See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.5.

oneOf.description: Text content delta from assistant response.
Tool use block received from API (input complete). Creates a ThreadItem.
Tool execution has begun (after permission check).

Tool execution completed with result.

`name` is carried here so StreamAccumulator and TUI consumers can reconstruct display state without maintaining their own call_id ‚Üí name map.
MCP tool call initiated (separate from builtin tools).

AgentStreamEvent.title: AgentStreamEvent

ApplyPatchToolType.description: How a model handles file editing / apply_patch tool.

oneOf.description: Shell-based, prompt instructions only (GPT-5, o3, o4-mini).

ApplyPatchToolType.title: ApplyPatchToolType

ApprovalDecision.description: TS uses `allow` / `deny` / `ask` for the canUseTool response flow.
ApprovalDecision.title: ApprovalDecision

ApprovalResolveParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlPermissionRequestSchema` response shape flipped ‚Äî here the SDK is *resolving* an approval request, so it's sent client‚Üíserver.

content_blocks.description: Optional content blocks (typically image attachments) the SDK client wants attached to the next user message. Mirrors TS `contentBlocks?: ContentBlockParam[]` on `PermissionAllowDecision` (`types/permissions.ts:183`) ‚Äî paste-image-during-AskUserQuestion or attachments alongside `MCPTool` answers ride this slot. Carried verbatim as `serde_json::Value` because the underlying `ContentBlockParam` is Anthropic-shaped; consumers translate per provider.

feedback.description: Optional feedback to inject back to the model.

permission_update.description: Optional permission update to persist to rules.

updated_input.description:
Optional rewritten tool input the SDK client supplies at approval time. When `Some`, the engine substitutes this for the model-emitted input before invoking the tool. Used by `AskUserQuestion` to ship user-selected `answers` (and optional `annotations`) back into the tool's data envelope.

Protocol mirror of `coco_tool_runtime::ToolPermissionResolution.updated_input` (the in-process equivalent for TUI mode). TS parity: `permissionDecision.updatedInput` at `services/tools/toolExecution.ts:1130-1131`. Consumed by `app/cli/src/sdk_server/approval_bridge.rs`.

permission_suggestions.description: Suggested permission updates the SDK can present to the user.

request_id.description: Unique correlation id (SDK must echo in `ApprovalResolve`).

CancelReason.description:
Why a turn was interrupted. Lets the TUI distinguish "user pressed Ctrl+C ‚Äî restore the input if conditions match" from "system cancelled the in-flight turn to make room for `/clear` / `/compact` / `/rewind` / shutdown / next submit ‚Äî leave the conversation alone".

Mirrors TS `abortController.signal.reason` discrimination at `REPL.tsx:3001`. New variants default to non-user-initiated semantics at the consumer (no auto-restore), so adding e.g. `BudgetExhausted` or `Timeout` later is additive without breaking the TUI handler.

oneOf.description: User-initiated cancel (Ctrl+C in the TUI, `control/interrupt` in the SDK). The only reason that may trigger auto-restore.

System pre-empted the in-flight turn so another session-level operation can run (Clear / Compact / Rewind / Shutdown / new SubmitInput). Auto-restore is suppressed ‚Äî the user did not request a rewind.
`context-1m-2025-08-07` beta ‚Äî 1M token context window. Wire name forced to `"context_1m"` (serde's snake_case treats digits as part of the preceding word and would emit `"context1m"`).
`interleaved-thinking-2025-05-14` beta. Also gates `redact-thinking-2026-02-12` (TS `betas.ts:272` reuses the same `modelSupportsISP` predicate for both).

Anthropic adaptive thinking ‚Äî server picks effort dynamically. Gates the convert layer's emission of `thinking: {"type":"adaptive"}` for `ReasoningEffort::Auto`. Without this capability, the convert layer omits the field entirely so the server-side default applies (avoids 400 from non-adaptive models that reject the value).

Known supporting models (Anthropic family): Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.6+, DeepSeek V4 (anthropic-compat).
`token-efficient-tools-2026-03-28` beta. Mutually exclusive with structured outputs.

Server-side `tool_reference` expansion (Anthropic beta `tool-search-tool-2025-10-19`). When set, the provider's API server expands `{type:"tool_reference",tool_name:X}` content blocks into inline `<functions>...</functions>` markup before the prompt reaches the model. Lets the client keep the `tools` array constant across turns (delayed tools carry `defer_loading: true`) and emit references inside `tool_result.content` instead of growing the tools list ‚Äî preserving prompt cache prefix across `ToolSearch` discoveries.

Provider-specific (Anthropic-only). The multi-provider default path is client-side promotion through `ToolAppState::discovered_tool_names`, which costs a cache break on the tools array but works on every provider.

Known supporting models: Claude Sonnet 4.5+, Opus 4+, GPT-5 (anthropic-compat). NOT supported on Haiku 4.5 / 3.5 / older 3-series.

Provider/model is known to work correctly with coco-rs's client-side `ToolSearch` promotion path (`discovered_tool_names` `AppStatePatch` + tools-array growth on the next turn).

**Per-model opt-in**, default **off** for unknown models. Rationale: a model that doesn't tolerate the growing tools array (legacy proxies, local quantized models with strict schema cache, ‚Ä¶) shouldn't be silently subjected to ToolSearch ‚Äî eager-loading every tool's full schema on turn 1 is the safe degradation. Set this capability in the registry (`builtin_models_partial`) once a model has been validated.

The runtime activation predicate is: ```text tool_search_active = Feature::ToolSearch && (ServerSideToolReference || ClientSideToolSearch) ``` When **both** capabilities are absent, the model lands in the "eager-load every tool, hide ToolSearch" state regardless of the user's `Feature::ToolSearch` setting.

No TS analogue ‚Äî TS only ships the server-side path and blacklists incompatible models via `DEFAULT_UNSUPPORTED_MODEL_PATTERNS`. coco-rs needs a positive capability for the client-side path because it works on every Provider, so "default-on" would mis-fire on local / custom model deployments that nobody has vetted.

ClientRequest.description:
Bidirectional control protocol ‚Äî client-initiated requests.

Each variant carries a unique `method` string used on the wire. The method is the discriminator; params are the variant-specific payload.

See `event-system-design.md` ¬ß5.1 for the 22 base variants and ¬ß5.4 for the 7 gap additions (`elicitation/resolve` is TS-aligned). 30 total.

oneOf.description:
Resolve a pending MCP elicitation request. Counterpart to the `ServerRequest` the agent sends when an MCP server needs structured user input (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.).

TS: `SDKControlElicitationRequestSchema` ‚Äî documented as a planned addition in `event-system-design.md` ¬ß5.4.
Query MCP server connection status. TS: `SDKControlMcpStatusRequestSchema`
Get context window usage breakdown. TS: `SDKControlGetContextUsageRequestSchema`
Reconnect a specific MCP server. TS: `SDKControlMcpReconnectRequestSchema`
Enable/disable a specific MCP server. TS: `SDKControlMcpToggleRequestSchema`
Reload all plugins from disk. TS: `SDKControlReloadPluginsRequestSchema`
Apply feature flag settings at runtime. TS: `SDKControlApplyFlagSettingsRequestSchema`

ClientRequest.title: ClientRequest

ClientRequestMethod.description:
Wire-method identifier for every `ClientRequest` variant.

Cross-language protocol constant exported to the JSON schema bundle so Python / other SDK codegens obtain the same vocabulary. Consumers should reference `ClientRequestMethod::SessionStart` rather than compare against raw wire strings.
ClientRequestMethod.title: ClientRequestMethod

CompactTrigger.description:
How compaction was triggered.

Stays in `coco-types` (rather than `coco-messages`) because `event::CompactionPhaseParams` references it; the rest of the message family lives in `coco-messages`.

Mirrors the TS taxonomy: manual `/compact`, threshold-based auto, PTL-413 reactive recovery, gap-based time-based microcompact, session-memory short-circuit (no LLM), and staged context-collapse commit.

CompactionPhase.description:
Sub-phase of a compaction in progress (TS `onCompactProgress`).

Mirrors the TS phase taxonomy at Tool.ts:150-156: - `HooksStart { hook_type }` for PreCompact / PostCompact / SessionStart - `Summarizing` for the LLM summarizer call - `Done` to clear the spinner

hook_type.description: Set when `phase == HooksStart`. None when not applicable.

post_tokens.description: Estimated tokens after compaction (resulting context size).

pre_tokens.description: Estimated tokens before compaction (post-strategy LLM input view). `None` for paths that do not measure (e.g. micro/time-based may skip when the savings are 0).

trigger.description:
Which strategy produced this compaction.

Mirrors the TS `tengu_compact.trigger` field. Older transcripts without this field default to `Auto`. Defaulted via serde so off-the-wire payloads from older SDK clients keep parsing.

ElicitationCompleteParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).

Emitted after an MCP server's elicitation request is resolved (either submitted or cancelled).

ElicitationResolveParams.description:
Params for `elicitation/resolve`.

Sent client‚Üíserver in response to a prior `ServerRequest` that asked the client to collect structured input on behalf of an MCP server (form values, OAuth tokens, etc.). The client populates `values` with the user's input and sets `approved=true`, or sets `approved=false` to reject the elicitation.

TS reference: `SDKControlElicitationRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts) ‚Äî TS uses a single bidirectional message that carries both request and response shapes; coco-rs splits them into a `ServerRequest` for the ask and this params struct for the reply.

approved.description: Whether the user approved the elicitation. If `false`, `values` is ignored and the MCP server sees a rejection.

mcp_server_name.description: Which MCP server the elicitation originated from. Echoed back so the agent can route the resolution to the right connection.

request_id.description: Correlation id matching the `ServerRequest` the agent sent.

values.description: The collected field values keyed by field name. Each value is an opaque JSON payload so typed/untyped fields share the wire format. Empty when `approved=false`.

ExpandedView.description: Which panel the TUI should have expanded in the task area. TS parity: `AppState.expandedView` in `AppStateStore.ts` (3 variants: `'none' | 'tasks' | 'teammates'`).

FilesPersistedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).

TS emits this when files are uploaded or persisted (e.g. after a successful `filesApi` operation).

HookCallbackParams.description: Invoke an SDK-registered hook callback. Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:366-371).

HookCallbackResponseParams.description: Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` response direction flipped ‚Äî client‚Üíserver reply to a prior `hook/callback` ServerRequest.

InitializeParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKControlInitializeRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:57-71).

Sent once at session start for capability negotiation. Carries hooks, SDK MCP servers, output format, system prompt, and agent definitions so the agent can construct its registries before the first turn.

agents.description: Custom agent definitions keyed by name.

append_system_prompt.description: Text appended to the default system prompt.

hooks.description: Hook callbacks keyed by event type.

prompt_suggestions.description: Enable prompt suggestions in the output stream.

sdk_mcp_servers.description: SDK-provided MCP server names (to skip env-configured ones).

InputTokenDetails.description: Input-side token breakdown ‚Äî mirrors `vercel_ai::InputTokenDetails` (which itself flattens `vercel_ai_provider::InputTokens`).

cache_read_tokens.description: Input tokens served from the prompt cache. Both OpenAI-compat (`prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`) and Anthropic (`cache_read_input_tokens`) expose this.

cache_write_tokens.description: Input tokens written to the prompt cache. **Only** the Anthropic wire shape exposes this (`cache_creation_input_tokens`); OpenAI-compat responses (DeepSeek, xAI, Groq, ‚Ä¶) have no equivalent field and the value stays 0 there. Matches upstream `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible`.

no_cache_tokens.description: Input tokens that did NOT come from the prompt cache. `input_tokens = no_cache_tokens + cache_read_tokens` when the provider reports both.

JsonRpcError.description: Error response payload. Mirrors JSON-RPC 2.0 error structure.
JsonRpcError.title: JsonRpcError

JsonRpcMessage.description: Top-level wire message. SDK clients send these over stdin; coco-rs writes these to stdout. Consumers dispatch on the `type` discriminator (NOT `method` ‚Äî JSON-RPC's `method` field is inside the inner payload).

oneOf.description: Request expecting a response. Correlates via `request_id`.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for correlating the response.

oneOf.description: Fire-and-forget notification (no response expected). `ServerNotification` is the usual payload in coco-rs.

JsonRpcMessage.title: JsonRpcMessage

JsonRpcNotification.description: Fire-and-forget notification. In coco-rs this is the primary outbound format for `ServerNotification` events (no `request_id`).
JsonRpcNotification.title: JsonRpcNotification

JsonRpcRequest.description: A JSON-RPC request wrapper. Holds the method name + params.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for correlating the response.

JsonRpcRequest.title: JsonRpcRequest

LocalCommandOutputParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).

TS emits this when the user runs a local bash command via the REPL `!` prefix (not a tool call). The `content` field is the command output; TS types it as the raw output structure (typically stdout/stderr).

root.description: Workspace root the prewarm anchored at.

started.description: IDs of language servers that successfully spawned (e.g. `["rust-analyzer", "gopls"]`).

McpConnectionStatus.description:
MCP server connection state on the wire.

Matches the TS `McpServerStatusSchema` enum at `coreSchemas.ts:167-173`: `'connected' | 'failed' | 'needs-auth' | 'pending' | 'disabled'`. `Disconnected` is a coco-rs extension used when the connection manager has no record of a named server.

oneOf.description: coco-rs extension: server name unknown to the connection manager.

McpRouteMessageParams.description: Route an MCP JSON-RPC message to an SDK-hosted server.

message.description: The raw JSON-RPC message to forward.

McpRouteMessageResponseParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlMcpMessageRequestSchema` response direction ‚Äî client‚Üíserver reply to a prior `mcp/routeMessage` ServerRequest.

message.description: JSON-RPC message response from the SDK-hosted MCP server.

McpServerInit.description: MCP server init entry (inline struct in TS).

MemoryDialogEntry.description: One row in the `/memory` file-picker overlay. Built by the slash dispatcher and shipped to the TUI via [`TuiOnlyEvent::OpenMemoryDialog`].

label.description: Display label shown in the picker row.

path.description: Filesystem path of the memory file (also the editor target).

scope.description: Scope tag (drives ordering and color hint).

MemoryDialogScope.description: Scope tag for a memory file picker entry. Mirrors `coco_commands::MemoryScope` ‚Äî kept in `coco-types` so the TUI can consume the event without depending on `coco-commands`.

oneOf.description: Enterprise / managed (typically read-only for the user).

ModelRole.description: Which purpose a model serves. Multiple roles can map to different models.

oneOf.description:
Plan-mode model. Used in two distinct contexts:

1. **Subagent role** ‚Äî when the built-in Plan subagent spawns (`SubagentType::Plan ‚Üí ModelRole::Plan` in `core/subagent/src/subagent_role.rs:34`), the spawn factory resolves the client via `client_for_role(Plan)`. 2. **Main-session plan-mode swap** ‚Äî when the leader enters `PermissionMode::Plan`, the engine swaps the active client to `client_for_role(Plan)` for the duration of plan mode. TS parity behavioral analogue: `getRuntimeMainLoopModel`'s `opusplan` ‚Üí Opus alias swap (`utils/model/model.ts:145-167`). coco-rs encodes this as a generic role slot so it works for any provider, not just Anthropic.

Unconfigured `models.plan` falls back to Main's spec via the chain in `runtime.rs:507`, and `client_for_role` short-circuits to the cached Main `Arc` ‚Äî both call sites degrade cleanly to "no swap" without spurious cache breaks.

Forked-agent spawn (AgentTool / SkillTool). Distinct from `Explore` ‚Äî Explore is an investigative subagent type that happens to often be a "small fast" model; Subagent is the generic spawn role used by tools/Agent and the swarm runtime.

ModelRole.title: ModelRole

ModelRoleChangedParams.description:
Payload for [`crate::ServerNotification::ModelRoleChanged`]. Carries the resolved binding (model + provider + thinking effort) that the TUI applies to `state.session.model_by_role[role]` and, when `role == Main`, also to `state.session.{model, provider, thinking_effort}` for the status bar.

Emitted by `tui_runner` after applying an in-memory override via `SessionRuntime::apply_role_override` / `apply_role_effort`. No persistence to settings.json ‚Äî that's the user's job.

effort.description: `None` ‚áí engine falls back to the model's `default_thinking_level`. `Some(_)` ‚áí explicit user choice.

ModelSpec.description: A resolved model identity: provider + model ID. Produced by coco-config, consumed by coco-inference.
ModelSpec.title: ModelSpec

NotificationMethod.description:
Wire-method identifier for every `ServerNotification` variant.

Cross-language protocol constant exported to the JSON schema bundle so Python / other SDK codegens obtain the same vocabulary. Consumers should reference `NotificationMethod::SessionStarted` rather than compare against raw wire strings.
NotificationMethod.title: NotificationMethod

reasoning_tokens.description: Output tokens spent on reasoning / thinking blocks. Already counted inside `output_tokens` and billed at the output rate. Sourced from OpenAI-compat's `completion_tokens_details.reasoning_tokens` or Anthropic's thinking-block usage where exposed.

text_tokens.description: Output tokens spent on plain text (non-reasoning). `output_tokens = text_tokens + reasoning_tokens` when the provider reports both.

PermissionAskChoice.description:
One option in a multi-choice permission dialog.

Used by `ToolCheckResult::Ask.choices` and surfaced on the wire via `PermissionDecision::Ask.choices`. The TUI renders one row per choice; the picked `value` is echoed back so the tool's `execute()` can branch on the user's selection.

description.description: Optional one-line explanation rendered under the label.

label.description: Short row label shown to the user.

value.description: Stable identifier echoed back in the approval response. Use kebab-case (`"yes-keep-context"`, `"yes-clear-context"`, `"no"`).

PermissionMode.description:
Permission mode (top-level because it's used by both message and permission modules).

Wire format is camelCase to match TS `PermissionModeSchema` at `coreSchemas.ts:337-347`: `z.enum(['default', 'acceptEdits', 'bypassPermissions', 'plan', 'dontAsk'])`. The serde aliases on the drifting variants accept legacy snake_case input so old session transcripts deserialize cleanly.

PermissionRuleSource.description: Source of a permission rule (ordered by priority: Session is most specific).

PermissionRuleValue.description: Permission rule value ‚Äî tool_pattern is a glob/wildcard expression. Examples: "Read", "Bash(git *)", "mcp__slack__*", "*"

PermissionUpdateDestination.description:
Destination for persisting permission updates.

Persistable destinations (`User`/`Project`/`LocalSettings`) write to disk; in-memory destinations (`Session`/`CliArg`/`Command`) live only for the running session. TS parity: same split as `persistPermissionUpdates` in `PermissionUpdate.ts`.

`Command` is reserved for rules contributed by an invoked command or skill's frontmatter (`allowed-tools:`). TS parity: `alwaysAllowRules.command` populated by `SkillTool` / `createGetAppStateWithAllowedTools`.

oneOf.description: Rules contributed by a command/skill frontmatter `allowed-tools`. In-memory only; cleared on session end.

PlanApprovalRequestedParams.description: A teammate's plan-approval request, surfaced to the team lead's TUI for approve/deny. Payload byte-matches TS `PlanApprovalRequestSchema` ‚Äî see `tools/ExitPlanModeTool/`.

plan_content.description: Plan text rendered from the teammate's plan file.

request_id.description: Correlation id carried back in the response envelope.

PluginInit.description: Plugin init entry (inline struct in TS).

ProviderApi.description: Which LLM provider implementation to use. Consumed by coco-config (ProviderInfo) and coco-inference (ProviderFactory).
ProviderApi.title: ProviderApi

ReasoningEffort.description: Reasoning effort. Ordered from "off" through numeric intensity. Provider-agnostic ‚Äî `thinking_convert` maps to per-provider wire shapes.

oneOf.description: Explicit "thinking off". Convert layer skips typed-effort emission; `level.options` may still carry an explicit-off wire toggle (e.g. DeepSeek's `{"thinking":{"type":"disabled"}}`).
"Let the provider decide". Convert layer omits reasoning fields; the server-side default applies. This is the default state when no thinking level is configured.

RequestId.description: Request identifier. Can be a string or integer per JSON-RPC 2.0. SDK clients typically use integers; coco-rs accepts both.
RequestId.title: RequestId

RequestUserInputParams.description: Ask the SDK to request user input (free-form or choice-list).

choices.description: Optional choice list; if present, the SDK should render a picker.

ServerNotification.description:
Protocol-level notifications visible to all consumers.

66 variants across 20 categories. See `event-system-design.md` Section 2. Each variant's wire method is generated together with the matching `NotificationMethod` discriminant.

oneOf.description: Session result (final usage, cost, stop reason).
Thread item updated (e.g. tool execution began).

LSP server pool finished prewarm. Fired once per session bootstrap (after `LspManagerAdapter::prewarm` completes), so the TUI status bar can show a `LSP` badge with the running-server count. Not emitted when `Feature::Lsp` is off ‚Äî `started` / `failed` are empty in that case.

Compaction phase progress (TS `onCompactProgress`). Drives the spinner text in the TUI / SDK runner so the user can see which sub-phase is active (PreCompact hooks ‚Üí summarize ‚Üí PostCompact hooks ‚Üí done).

Durable plan-item / V1 todo snapshot ‚Äî emitted after `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate`/`TodoWrite` tools mutate state so the TUI can refresh its panel without pulling the store directly. TS parity: `notifyTasksUpdated` subscriber callback in `utils/tasks.ts`.
Team lead received a plan-approval request from a teammate (via mailbox). The TUI surfaces this as a modal overlay. TS parity: `ExitPlanModeV2Tool.ts:137-141` teammate request flow.

A role's binding (model + provider + effort) changed in-memory via the picker or `Ctrl+T`. Carries the resolved fields the TUI needs to refresh its `model_by_role` cache and, for `Main`, status-bar fields (`model`, `provider`, `thinking_effort`).
Hook execution progress (TS gap P1 ‚Äî stdout/stderr streaming).
Output from a user-executed local command (REPL `!` prefix). Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).
Files persisted to disk (file upload/snapshot completion). Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).
MCP elicitation completed (form submission or cancellation). Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).
Tool use summary from background haiku summarization. Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).
Tool execution progress (bash/powershell long-running). Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659). Sent at most once per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`.

ServerNotification.title: ServerNotification

ServerRequest.description:
Bidirectional control protocol ‚Äî server-initiated requests.

The agent sends these to SDK clients when it needs a decision or input (permission approval, user question, hook callback, MCP routing). The SDK client must reply via the corresponding `ClientRequest` variant.

oneOf.description: Ask the SDK client to approve or deny a tool use. Matches TS `SDKControlPermissionRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:108-121). Expected response: `ClientRequest::ApprovalResolve`.
Ask the user a question via the SDK client (e.g. multiple choice). Expected response: `ClientRequest::UserInputResolve`.
Route an MCP JSON-RPC message to the SDK-hosted MCP server. Matches TS `SDKControlMcpMessageRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:377-381). Expected response: `ClientRequest::McpRouteMessageResponse`.
Invoke an SDK-registered hook callback. Matches TS `SDKHookCallbackRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:366-371). Expected response: `ClientRequest::HookCallbackResponse`.
Notify the SDK that a previously-sent ServerRequest should be cancelled.

ServerRequest.title: ServerRequest

ServerRequestMethod.description:
Wire-method identifier for every `ServerRequest` variant.

Cross-language protocol constant exported to the JSON schema bundle so Python / other SDK codegens obtain the same vocabulary. Consumers should reference `ServerRequestMethod::HookCallback` rather than compare against raw wire strings.
ServerRequestMethod.title: ServerRequestMethod

SessionResultParams.description: Matches TS `SDKResultMessageSchema` (coreSchemas.ts:1407-1451). TS has two subtype variants (success/error) unified here with `is_error` flag.

errors.description: Error variant: list of error strings.

num_api_calls.description: coco-rs extension: num_api_calls for observability (not in TS).

permission_denials.description: Permission denials accumulated during the session.

result.description: Success variant: the agent's final result text.

initial_prompt.description: Optional initial user prompt to run immediately after start.

SessionStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKSystemMessageSchema` with subtype 'init' (coreSchemas.ts:1457-1494). Sent once at session startup; carries the full bootstrap context the SDK consumer needs to render a UI.

agents.description: Agent type names available for Agent tool spawning.

lsp_active.description: `true` when at least one LSP server is healthy at session startup (`Feature::Lsp` on + adapter `is_connected() = true`). Used by the TUI status bar to render an "LSP" badge. Default `false` keeps legacy SDK clients backward-compatible.

slash_commands.description: Slash commands available in this session.

version.description: Release version of the coco-rs binary.

oneOf.description: Turn completed, waiting for user input.
Waiting for user action (approval, question, elicitation).

model.description: None means revert to the default model.

SetPermissionModeParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKControlSetPermissionModeRequestSchema` (controlSchemas.ts:127-134).

TS carries an additional `ultraplan: boolean` field for the CCR web-UI refinement flow. coco-rs intentionally skips Ultraplan (see CLAUDE.md "Plan Mode ‚Äî Skip Ultraplan (CCR Web UI) Only"), so that field is omitted here ‚Äî SDK clients targeting coco-rs should not send it.

SetThinkingParams.description: Matches TS `SDKControlSetMaxThinkingTokensRequestSchema` + ThinkingConfig. TS only carries `max_thinking_tokens: number | null`; coco-rs uses the richer `ThinkingLevel` from coco-types which includes effort level and per-provider options.

SlashCommandStatusKind.description:
Categorization of a `SlashCommandStatus` payload. Each variant maps to a `slash.status.*` key in the TUI locale catalog.

Wire format intentionally tagged so SDK clients can render their own localized strings instead of consuming the TUI's English fallback.

oneOf.description: Registered command has no `handler` ‚Äî typically a plugin contribution stub that wasn't bridged.
Handler returned `Err`. `error` is the formatted error message.
Handler returned `CommandResult::Prompt` with no text parts.
Handler returned `CommandResult::OpenDialog`, but coco-rs has not yet bound this dialog kind to a TUI overlay. `dialog_kind` is a human-readable label like "memory file selector".
`/permissions allow` invoked with no tool name. Dispatcher-side usage hint ‚Äî the TUI translates via `slash.permissions.usage_allow`.
`/permissions deny` invoked with no tool name. Dispatcher-side usage hint ‚Äî the TUI translates via `slash.permissions.usage_deny`.

TaskCompletedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1694-1713). TS calls this `task/notification`; coco-rs uses `task/completed` as the wire method for brevity, but fields match TS exactly.

TaskListStatus.description: Task status wire format ‚Äî matches TS `TaskStatusSchema` (`utils/tasks.ts:69-74`). **Distinct** from [`crate::TaskStatus`], which is the 6-variant running-task lifecycle enum.

TaskPanelChangedParams.description: Snapshot of the task panel state ‚Äî tools emit this post-mutation so the TUI can redraw without reaching into `ToolAppState` directly.

TaskProgressParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1750-1767). In TS, `description` and `usage` are required; other fields optional. The `workflow_progress` field carries the streaming state of local_workflow tasks ‚Äî a delta batch of workflow state changes.

TaskStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskStartedMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1715-1733). TS has `description` required and `task_type` optional.

ThinkingLevel.description:
Unified thinking configuration for all providers.

`effort` carries provider-agnostic intent ‚Äî `Disable`, `Auto`, or one of the numeric levels (`Minimal`..`XHigh`). Provider-specific wire toggles (e.g. DeepSeek's `{"thinking":{"type":"enabled"}}`) flow through `options` verbatim.

Semantic states: * `Disable` ‚Äî explicit "thinking off"; emit explicit-off signals where the provider supports them, otherwise omit reasoning fields. * `Auto`    ‚Äî "let the provider decide"; omit reasoning fields so the server-side default applies. * `Minimal`..`XHigh` ‚Äî explicit numeric efforts; emitted via the provider's typed reasoning channel.

budget_tokens.description: Token budget ‚Äî universal for budget-based providers.

effort.description: Reasoning effort ‚Äî universal across all providers.

ThreadItem.description:
Semantic representation of a conversation thread item. Produced by `StreamAccumulator` from `AgentStreamEvent` sequences. Used in `ServerNotification::ItemStarted / ItemUpdated / ItemCompleted`.

See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.6.
ThreadItem.title: ThreadItem

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2): - Bash ‚Üí `CommandExecution` - Edit/Write ‚Üí `FileChange` - WebSearch ‚Üí `WebSearch` - mcp__* ‚Üí `McpToolCall` - Agent/Task ‚Üí `Subagent` - all others ‚Üí `ToolCall` - text content ‚Üí `AgentMessage` - thinking ‚Üí `Reasoning` - errors ‚Üí `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool ‚Üí command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools ‚Üí file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

TodoRecord.description: A TodoWrite item ‚Äî byte-matches TS `TodoItemSchema` (no `id` field, positional identity).

TokenUsage.description:
Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).

**Shape mirrors `vercel_ai::LanguageModelUsage`** ‚Äî the high-level SDK type at `vercel-ai/ai/src/types/usage.rs`. Field names and nesting are identical so a value can move across the seam without information loss. `i64` is used in place of vercel-ai's `Option<u64>` to match the rest of coco-rs's token-count idiom; "not reported" surfaces as `0`.

Backward-compat: every nested field is `#[serde(default)]`, so old session JSON without the breakdown fields parses cleanly with breakdown values defaulting to 0.

input_tokens.description: Total input tokens consumed (= `no_cache + cache_read + cache_write` when all three are reported).

output_tokens.description: Total output tokens consumed (= `text + reasoning` when both are reported).

total_tokens.description: `input_tokens + output_tokens`. Convenience aggregate matching vercel-ai's `LanguageModelUsage.total_tokens`. Default to 0 when not provided; `Self::total()` recomputes if needed.

ToolProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).

Long-running tool progress (Bash, PowerShell). TS throttles emission to ‚â§1 per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`. coco-rs StreamAccumulator may emit this independently from `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseStarted`.

ToolUseSummaryParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Background Haiku-based summary of a batch of tool uses. TS uses this to compress verbose tool output before it's displayed or archived.

TuiOnlyEvent.description:
TUI-exclusive events.

These events are dropped by SDK and App-Server consumers. They drive overlays, toasts, and UI-only state transitions that are not part of the protocol contract.

Per `event-system-design.md` Section 1.7, the design listed this type as owned by `coco-tui`. Since `CoreEvent::Tui(TuiOnlyEvent)` is part of the envelope enum defined in `coco-types`, the type itself must live in `coco-types` to avoid a cyclic dependency. The semantic contract (TUI-only, never sent to SDK) is preserved via consumer dispatch rules in `StreamAccumulator` and `handle_core_event()`.

21 variants (20 from design ¬ß4.1 + 1 coco-rs extension).

oneOf.description:
Permission approval overlay needed.

When `choices` is `Some`, the TUI should render a multi-choice list (e.g. ExitPlanMode's keep/clear/cancel) instead of the default yes/no buttons. Picked `value` is echoed back via `UserCommand::ApprovalResponse.updated_input` as a `{ ..original_input, user_choice: "<value>" }` JSON object so the tool's `execute()` can branch on the choice. TS parity: `ExitPlanModePermissionRequest.tsx:691-704` option grid.

`original_input` carries the raw tool input so the TUI can splice the picked `user_choice` into it verbatim before sending the approval response ‚Äî mirrors `QuestionAsked.input`. `None` keeps the wire compact when no choices are present.

AskUserQuestion overlay needed. `input` carries the full tool input dict (the `questions[]` array, etc.) verbatim so the TUI renders the rich multi-question UI (multiSelect, preview, notes) matching TS `AskUserQuestionPermissionRequest.tsx`. The TUI echoes the answer payload back via `UserCommand::ApprovalResponse` with `updated_input = Some({...input, answers, annotations})`.

file_paths.description: Path strings of changed files in display order. Empty when `files_changed == 0`. TS source: `DiffStats.filesChanged: string[]` (`utils/fileHistory.ts:55-61`). Used by the rewind picker to render basenames per row and the selected-file label on the confirm screen. `#[serde(default)]` keeps the wire backwards-compatible with older producers that didn't populate it.

oneOf.description: Cron job disabled by circuit breaker.

Streaming tool input delta (typing effect).

# Status: reserved scaffolding, not yet wired

The TUI has a handler (`server_notification_handler::handle_tui_only`) that appends the delta to `ToolExecution.streaming_input` for a typing-effect display, but **no producer currently emits this variant** in coco-rs.

The inference layer's `StreamEvent::ToolCallDelta` (a different type, internal to `coco-inference`) is fully accumulated into the complete tool input before the engine emits `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseQueued` with the finalized input. Consumers see the complete input at once.

Future work to wire this up would require the inference layer to forward the partial JSON fragments alongside the accumulation, and the engine to emit them here as `CoreEvent::Tui(ToolCallDelta { ... })`.

# Why keep it in TuiOnlyEvent (not AgentStreamEvent)

Per `event-system-design.md` ¬ß3.3: partial JSON deltas serve a purely UI display purpose (typing effect) and the SDK does not need them ‚Äî `ToolUseQueued` already contains the complete input. Promoting to `AgentStreamEvent` would burden SDK consumers with partial JSON they must re-assemble, with no behavioral benefit.

Rewind completed ‚Äî TUI truncates messages and restores input state. coco-rs extension: UI-only because it carries TUI-specific identifiers for message truncation and input repopulation. Out-of-band from the design's `rewind/completed` ServerNotification which carries protocol metadata only.

files_changed.description: Number of files restored (0 if conversation-only).

target_message_id.description: UUID of the target user message. Empty = code-only rewind.

oneOf.description: Local slash-command produced a `CommandResult::Text` (or `Compact { display_text }`). Surfaced as a system-role chat message so it reads inline with the transcript. `text` is the pre-rendered body ‚Äî never translated, since it carries the handler's actual output (often command-specific status / git output / prompt preview).

Dispatcher-side breadcrumb for slash commands the runtime couldn't fully execute (missing handler, handler error, empty Prompt body, dialog wiring pending). The TUI translates `kind` via the i18n catalog before rendering.

Tell the TUI to open the rewind picker overlay. Emitted when the slash dispatcher resolves `/rewind` from the command palette (typed `/rewind` is intercepted earlier in `update/edit.rs` without round-tripping through `CoreEvent`). The TUI consumes the current `session.messages` to build the picker.

Tell the TUI to open the `/memory` file-picker overlay. The slash dispatcher pre-builds entries via `coco_commands::handlers::memory_dialog::MemoryDialogHandler::entries` and carries them through here so the TUI doesn't recompute paths. On selection the TUI creates the file (`mode wx` semantics), opens `$VISUAL || $EDITOR`, and emits a system message; on cancel it emits "Cancelled memory editing". TS parity: `commands/memory/memory.tsx`.

Result of a prompt-mode `!`-prefixed bash submission. The TUI folds this into a `MessageContent::BashOutput` chat message keyed by the same `user_message_id` as the matching `BashInput`. Emitted by the CLI bridge after running the command via `coco_shell::ShellExecutor`. TS parity: `LocalShellTask` completion.

exit_code.description: Process exit code; non-zero shades the output red.

output.description: Merged stdout + stderr, already truncated to a reasonable display size by the bridge.

user_message_id.description: Shared id of the bash input/output pair so rewind groups them.

oneOf.description: Confirmation that a prompt-mode `#`-prefixed memory entry was appended to a CLAUDE.md file. The TUI surfaces a toast and the `MemoryInput` chat message that was already pushed locally.

path.description: Path of the file that was appended to (project / user scope).

oneOf.description: Tell the TUI to open the provider-grouped model picker. Emitted when the slash dispatcher resolves `/model` with no args (typed `/model` from input bar). The TUI consumes the current `state.session.model` to mark the "current" entry.

TuiOnlyEvent.title: TuiOnlyEvent

reason.description: Why the turn was interrupted. `None` only on legacy transcripts that pre-date this field; new senders always populate it. The TUI handler treats `None` as `SystemPreempt` (conservative ‚Äî no auto-restore on unknown reason).

answer.description: The user's answer to the `AskUserQuestion` prompt.

description: JSON Schema bundle for the coco-rs SDK protocol types. Source: coco-rs/common/types. Generated by `cargo run -p coco-types --features schema --example export_schema`. Composition: a small set of entry-point types + every type they transitively reference via `$ref` (collected by `schemars::schema_for!` and flattened into `definitions`). To add a new type, ensure it is reachable from an existing entry; only add a new entry if it is a true root.
title: coco-app-server-protocol
